what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that records what the confirmed official Claude channel mechanism means for the team's build, enumerating the remaining fork tasks and reliability caveats.
observations: The live chat path app /chat-send → cc_channels push → session is confirmed working with a test that showed delivered:true.
company_channel.mjs is described as the working reference the fork builds on: it declares claude/channel, emits a notification, and exposes a reply tool.
The fork's listed jobs are: (a) reply path to the browser via SSE, (b) register the app as a pushable member, (c) member picker + role resolution for 'the lead', (d) project switcher.
Reliability notes include keeping the lead session persistent, gating the sender, expecting batched-to-turn delivery, and building custom delivered/seen confirmation.
The team is on Linux/WSL, so GitHub issue #46125 (Windows-related) does not apply.
Sources cited are code.claude.com docs and GitHub issues #41733, #3174, and #46125.
standouts: Confirmation that the injected test delivered:true validates the official mechanism end-to-end.
company_channel.mjs is positioned as the local working reference for the fork.
Explicit four-part task list for the fork, including SSE browser reply path and member/role resolution.
Batched-to-turn delivery caveat and instruction to build own delivered/seen confirmation.
conventions: Uses ledger frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Markdown body uses a top-level heading and bullet lists.
References external documentation URLs and GitHub issue numbers inline.
notes: A build-impact note confirming the live chat channel path works and enumerating the fork's remaining SSE/reply/member/role tasks plus reliability caveats.
questions: What exactly is the fork and how does it relate to company_channel.mjs?
What does 'register the app as a pushable member' involve technically?
What is the scope and behavior of the 'project switcher'?
What is the definition of 'the lead' role and how is it resolved?
What do GitHub issues #41733, #3174, and #46125 specifically describe?
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to convert confirmation of an official channel mechanism into a concrete, actionable work list for the fork while preserving reliability and environment-specific caveats.
novelty: It treats an internal company_channel.mjs module as a working reference for an upstream-style fork, mixing official documentation with local implementation notes.
contribution: Captures a snapshot of confirmed working behavior, a prioritized task list for browser replies, pushable membership, member/role resolution, and project switching, plus environment-specific reliability caveats that a fused system would keep as implementation context.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item summarizing that the app-to-channel push chat path is the official mechanism, listing fork tasks and reliability notes for browser replies, membership, role resolution, and project switching.
intention_for_embedding: To record the build implications of the confirmed Claude channel mechanism and the remaining work needed in the fork.
